facts_disclosed: Driving in silence now — podcasts feel like noise

turns.content: you ever notice how loud everything is all the time like even when youre alone theres always something making sound
Yeah, that's a good observation — between phones, TVs, music, notifications, true quiet is pretty rare for most people. What's got you thinking about that?

so i been doing these long hauls right and lately i just drive with nothing on like no radio no nothing just the sound of the engine and the road and the wind and at first it was weird because the cab felt empty but now i actually prefer it you know
That's interesting. A lot of long-distance drivers say they need something playing to stay alert. What changed for you?

i think i realized all that audio was just filling up my head with other peoples thoughts and other peoples stories and other peoples opinions and i wasnt leaving any room for my own you know like my brain was always processing somebody elses content instead of processing my own life and my own decisions

That's a really profound insight. There's something to that — constant input can keep you busy without actually helping you think. Are you working through something specific in the quiet, or is it more of a general clarity thing?

bit of both i guess i got a lot going on right now with work stuff and where i want to be in 5 years and family things and when i had noise going all the time i could kind of ignore all that because there was always another episode to listen to or another show to start but now in the silence its just me and the road and whatever is actually in my head and some of it is uncomfortable but at least its real you know
It takes guts to sit with the uncomfortable stuff instead of drowning it out. Most people reach for their phone the second things get quiet. How long have you been doing the silent drives?

maybe a month now and its not every run sometimes on a short day haul ill put on some music kompa stuff you know haitian music but for the long night drives especially its just silence and the highway lights going by and i think more clearly at night anyway theres something about 2am on an empty interstate that makes everything feel more honest

2am on an empty interstate with nothing but your thoughts — that does sound like it would strip everything down to what's real. Has anything come out of all that thinking? Any clarity on the decisions you mentioned?

not really like not definite answers but i feel like im at least asking myself the right questions now instead of just pushing everything down and filling the time with distractions like i spent years just consuming consuming consuming and not once stopping to ask myself wait what do i actually want and now im asking and the answer is i dont know yet but at least im asking sa k pase thats progress right

That's absolutely progress. Knowing the right questions is half the battle — the answers usually come once you've sat with them long enough. And the fact that you made a conscious choice to create space for that kind of thinking says something about where you are right now.

yeah i think so too and its funny because the quiet used to scare me like when i first started driving years ago silence in the cab felt lonely and depressing but now it feels like i dont know like freedom almost like the silence belongs to me and nobody else is in it telling me what to think or buy or believe its just me and the engine and the dark and whatever comes up comes up

There's a big difference between lonely silence and chosen silence. It sounds like you've crossed over from one to the other. That's a real shift — when quiet stops feeling like emptiness and starts feeling like space.

mwen fatige though like emotionally not just physically all this thinking is tiring in a different way you know but i think its the kind of tired that means youre actually doing something important anyway im pulling into a rest area outside of knoxville gonna try to sleep for a few hours before the morning delivery bondye bon thanks for listening to me ramble about silence which is kind of ironic when you think about it

Ha, rambling about the beauty of silence — there is some irony there. But hey, sometimes you need to talk through the quiet to understand what its giving you. Get some rest in Knoxville and keep asking those questions. The answers will come when they're ready. Safe travels.
